title: Error: wait for container - wsarecv: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond.
body:
### Issue Description

I'm getting this error message after ~3 minutes of doing nothing in a newly created container:

```
 user  podman run --rm -it ubuntu /bin/bash
root@d7f1fc49b833:/# time="2025-07-31T10:59:41+02:00" level=error msg="Removing container d7f1fc49b833be0d29aa4462f533d42b1c9bc2e377d2bf09794b0e564cf15524: Delete \"http://d/v5.5.2/libpod/containers/d7f1fc49b833be0d29aa4462f533d42b1c9bc2e377d2bf09794b0e564cf15524?force=false&volumes=true\": read tcp 127.0.0.1:53372->127.0.0.1:60640: wsarecv: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond."
Error: wait for container: Post "http://d/v5.5.2/libpod/containers/d7f1fc49b833be0d29aa4462f533d42b1c9bc2e377d2bf09794b0e564cf15524/wait": read tcp 127.0.0.1:53372->127.0.0.1:60640: wsarecv: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond.
```

This also appears occasionally when trying to build devcontainers.

I'm behind a corporate proxy (ZScaler) and VPN (Cisco AnyConnect), which causes issues here and there, I suspect this could be related. A ticket at our local IT brought me to nowhere.

Any ideas what this is causing and how to debug and fix this?

Thanks!

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. `podman run --rm -it ubuntu /bin/bash`
2. wait 3 minutes
3. see it crash/timeout


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: wait for container: Post "http://d/v5.5.2/libpod/containers/d7f1fc49b833be0d29aa4462f533d42b1c9bc2e377d2bf09794b0e564cf15524/wait": read tcp 127.0.0.1:53372->127.0.0.1:60640: wsarecv: A connection attempt failed because the connected party did not properly respond after a period of time, or established connection failed because connected host has failed to respond.
```

### Describe the results you expected

I'd expect that the sessions remains intact.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Windows 11, latest Podman Desktop, ZScaler corporate proxy & Cisco AnyConnect VPN, wsl mirrored network enabled

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Interesting finding: When using `networkingMode=nat` in .wslconfig, it works. BUT: I don't want to use nat mode, mirroring has significant advantages when using proxies etc, see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/wsl/networking#mirrored-mode-networking.

See also https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13317
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
